title: Scope & Arrows 🏹
description: Understand where variables live, and learn the arrow function shortcut.

slides.title: Variable Scope 🗺️

quizData.question: What happens if you try to use a variable OUTSIDE the curly braces where it was created?

options.text: Dart throws an error because the variable is "out of scope" and no longer exists.

quizData.explanation: Variables are destroyed as soon as the code leaves the set of curly braces {} in which they were created.

slides.title: Global Variables 🌍
slides.content:
## Seeing Everything

If you create a variable OUTSIDE of any function (even outside `main`), it becomes a **Global Variable**.
Every single function in your file can see it and change it.

```dart
int highScore = 1000; // Global!

void increaseScore() {
  highScore += 50; // Perfectly valid!
}
```
*Note: Use globals sparingly, as they can make tracking bugs difficult!*

slides.title: Arrow Functions => 🏹
slides.content:
## The Ultimate Shortcut

If a function possesses EXACTLY one line of code that just returns a value, there is an amazing shortcut called the **Arrow Function**.

Instead of braces `{}` and the word `return`, you just shoot an arrow `=>` at the answer!

**Normal:** `int add(int a, int b) { return a + b; }`
**Arrow:** `int add(int a, int b) => a + b;`

quizData.question: When can you use the `=>` arrow function syntax?

options.text: Only when the function has EXACTLY one expression to return.
Any time, even if the function has 10 lines of code.

quizData.explanation: Arrow functions are syntactic sugar specifically designed for single-line return statements.

slides.title: See it in Action
slides.content: Look how incredibly clean arrow functions look!
